warnings: Backend uses renormalization scale = vev, no RG running or daisy resummation.
Gauge dependence of CW+thermal effective potential is an order-of-magnitude theory uncertainty on the GW spectrum (1707.06765).
xi=1 common-temperature assumption; dark dof (A':3, scalar:1) added to g_* internally by the backend.
f_peak_est is a template-independent order-of-magnitude anchor, NOT the true spectral peak (owned by pta-agent).
Strong-supercooling regime: very large alpha for low g_D; alpha varies over many orders of magnitude across the viable window.
approximations: High-temperature (small field/T) polynomial expansion of the effective potential.
Gaussian approximation for false-vacuum fraction / percolation.
Scalar and Goldstone contributions neglected in input (subleading; CW quartic fixed internally).

backend_choice.reason: Classically scale-invariant single-field model, masses prop. to one vev, no tree mass term => matches the selection rule.

independent.v: Dark-sector vacuum expectation value [GeV]. FOPT scan axis.
independent.g_D: U(1)_D gauge coupling [dimensionless]. FOPT scan axis.

dependent.lambda_Phi: CW-fixed scalar quartic; computed internally by the backend (V_eff'(v)=0). Never scanned.
